subject: Re: KeyNotFoundException during Ctf refinement of tilt series in M
body:
Hi All,

I think I figured out what my issue was. I have two species that I'm 
refining. One of the two is not at a high enough resolution for tilt series 
ctf refinement and is excluded via the resolution threshold (species 1). 
The other species is at a healthy 7Å and is used for ctf refinement 
(species 2). 

However, in some tilt series I have no particles of species 2. After a bit 
of digging it turns out that M was crashing when trying to access particles 
of species 2 from those tilt series for ctf refinement (things run fine 
without ctf refinement).

Adding a 'fake particle' for species 2 to these tilt series fixed the 
issue. 
However, a quicker (and cleaner) fix was to increase the number in 'only 
use series with at least x particles', so that tilt series that only had 
particles for species 1 are ignored.

Cheers,
Tom

Op zaterdag 27 maart 2021 om 22:54:04 UTC schreef tom.de...@gmail.com:

> Hi All, 
>
> I have been refining sub-tomogram averages in M and ran into the attached 
> KeyNotFoundException error for two tilt series when enabling ctf refinement 
> (defocus only). It seems M can't find something in the .xml file? Has 
> anyone seen this  before? Any tips for troubleshooting would be greatly 
> appreciated.
>
> Many thanks,
> Tom
>
>
>
>
>
